commands.repair_reason: - [P2] The remaining blocker is a narrow mechanical repair to clear stale diagnostic context and add focused regression coverage; the broader API direction can remain a maintainer decision after that fix.; Needs attention: The patch introduces a concrete privacy/security-boundary concern because stale caller-supplied context can be exposed to diagnostic subscribers on later runs.; Review finding: [P1] Clear clientCo... (sha=0e12371e95f6508fbda39d5ce0b5e86a2716384c)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an optional gateway `agent` `clientContext` request field, normalizes it, stores it in diagnostic session state, emits it on selected diagnostic events, and regenerates Swift protocol models.

PR surface: Source +182, Tests +209, Other +4. Total +395 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: seed a context on a session, start a later same-session run whose normalized context is undefined, then emit `session.state` or `message.queued`; the helper returns without clearing the prior context.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blocker is a narrow mechanical repair to clear stale diagnostic context and add focused regression coverage; the broader API direction can remain a maintainer decision after that fix.

target.title: feat(gateway): forward generic clientContext onto diagnostic events
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a generic, plugin-agnostic way for a gateway client to attach opaque run context that plugins can read off the diagnostic event stream.

The gateway `agent` request gains an optional `clientContext` object. It is bounded + normalized, seeded once onto the per-session diagnostic state, and then carried onto every `message.queued` / `session.state` event for that run — the same shared-state propagation that already gives a later `message.queued` its `sessionKey`. Core never inspects the contents and learns no plugin-specific names; a plugin (e.g. an OTel/tracing bridge) interprets the bag via a `schemaVersion` it owns.

This is the upstreamable core half of an attribution feature: a downstream orchestrator was launching gateway `agent` runs but the bridge plugin (which only observes diagnostic events) had no way to read the upstream identity, so spans were mis-attributed. With this change the orchestrator's context reaches the bridge through the diagnostic stream.

### What's in it

- `src/infra/diagnostic-client-context.ts` — bounded JSON normalizer: size (8 KB), depth (4), and key (64) caps; prototype-key sanitization; deterministic key ordering; whole-bag drop on cap/validation failure (never partial).
- `clientContext?: DiagnosticClientContext` on the `message.queued` and `session.state` event types.
- `clientContext` field on `SessionState` (+ carried in `mergeSessionState`); emitters write `state.clientContext`.
- `setDiagnosticSessionClientContext(ref, ctx)` seeder in `src/logging/diagnostic.ts`.
- Additive optional `clientContext` param on `AgentParamsSchema` (`packages/gateway-protocol`); the schema stays `additionalProperties: false` (no protocol version bump — purely additive).
- The gateway `agent` handler normalizes `request.clientContext` and seeds it before dispatch.

### Design notes

- **Plugin-agnostic:** core contains no consumer-specific names; the bag is opaque and the discriminator (`schemaVersion`) lives inside it, owned by the plugin.
- **Bounded:** caps protect the diagnostic stream and prompt-cache determinism (deterministic ordering) from unbounded/hostile input; out-of-bounds input is dropped (field omitted), never truncated mid-structure.
- **Inert without context:** `normalizeDiagnosticClientContext(undefined)` → `undefined` → seeder no-ops; events omit the field. No behavior change for existing runs.

## Verification

Ran in a clean worktree off `main`:

- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json` (covers `src/**` + `packages/**`) → clean
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/diagnostic-client-context.test.ts` → 10/10
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/logging/diagnostic-client-context-events.test.ts` → 3/3 (seed → `session.state` + `message.queued` inheritance)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/gateway-protocol/src/schema/agent.test.ts` → 4/4 (incl. new clientContext acceptance)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/logging/diagnostic.test.ts` → 68/68 (emitter regression)
- oxlint + oxfmt on all touched files → clean

CI covers the full suite.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: A gateway client can attach opaque `clientContext` to an `agent` run; it reaches plugins via `message.queued` / `session.state` diagnostic events, seeded once and inherited for the whole run. Absent/oversized context is a no-op.

Real environment tested: Exercised end-to-end through a downstream OTel bridge plugin against a live gateway: an orchestrator sent `clientContext` on `agent` runs and the bridge attributed the resulting spans/traces to the upstream identity (previously they fell back to local defaults). The unit tests above reproduce the seed→inherit mechanism deterministically.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/diagnostic-client-context.test.ts src/logging/diagnostic-client-context-events.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/gateway-protocol/src/schema/agent.test.ts src/logging/diagnostic.test.ts`

Evidence after fix: tsgo core clean; new tests 10/10, 3/3, 4/4; `diagnostic.test.ts` 68/68; oxlint/oxfmt clean. In the live bridge run, the root span carried the upstream identity and run/issue ids forwarded via `clientContext`; with no `clientContext` the span attribution was unchanged.

Observed result after fix: `clientContext` is carried on both `message.queued` and `session.state` (including a later `message.queued` that only specifies a `sessionId`, via the shared session state); omitted entirely when no context is supplied or it exceeds the bounds.

What was not tested: the full CI matrix locally (left to CI); the downstream bridge/plugin itself is out of this repo's scope.


---

### Real behavior proof (live)

`Behavior addressed:` A gateway client attaches opaque `clientContext` to an `agent` run; it reaches plugins via `message.queued` / `session.state` diagnostic events, seeded once and inherited for the whole run. Absent/oversized context is a no-op.

`Real environment tested:` Yes — a live OpenClaw gateway carrying this seeding logic, driven by a real upstream orchestrator (Paperclip) that sets `clientContext`, with an OTel bridge plugin reading the diagnostic stream and exporting to Tempo.

`Exact steps or command run after this patch:` Triggered a real orchestrator-managed run through the gateway, then read the gateway journal and queried Tempo.

`Evidence after fix:` Redacted gateway runtime log for run `e0f1ea4f-f440-4097-b8fc-1ee7da341dc0`:

```
[ws] ⇄ res ✓ agent ... runId=e0f1ea4f-f440-4097-b8fc-1ee7da341dc0
[bridge] event: session.state sessionKey: agent:main:paperclip-conductor
[bridge] started root span for ... session: e0f1ea4f-... agent: Conductor
[bridge] event: model.call.completed sessionKey: agent:main:paperclip-conductor
[bridge] ended upstream root span for session: agent:main:paperclip-conductor
```

Tempo trace `75839d85fe1df83e0c62370b7040410`, found by `{.prov.upstream.run_id="e0f1ea4f-..."}` and `{.session.id="e0f1ea4f-..."}`; root span attributes include `session.id=e0f1ea4f-...`, `prov.upstream.run_id=e0f1ea4f-...`, `prov.upstream.issue_id=53ce84e5-...`, `outcome=completed`.

`Observed result after fix:` The `clientContext` supplied on the `agent` request was carried through the diagnostic stream and consumed by the plugin; with no `clientContext` the attribution was unchanged. Full end-to-end write-up: https://github.com/arniesaha/agentweave/issues/238#issuecomment-4657108617

`What was not tested:` the full CI matrix locally (left to CI); the downstream plugin itself is out of this repo's scope.
target.author: arniesaha

commands.author: TurboTheTurtle

target.title: fix: honor parent session flag for prompts
target.body:
## Summary
- resolve prompt session names through parent/global command flags like the other session subcommands
- add regression coverage for `codex -s <name> prompt ...`
- drop the release-owned changelog edit from this PR

Fixes #355.

## Validation
- `pnpm run build:test && node --test dist-test/test/cli.test.js --test-name-pattern "prompt resolves named session"`
- `pnpm run format:check`
- manual CLI proof with built `dist/cli.js`

## Proof
Focused regression proof:

```text
✔ prompt resolves named session from parent -s flag
ℹ tests 78
ℹ pass 78
```

Manual real CLI proof for `-s` before `prompt`:

```text
command: HOME=<tmp> node dist/cli.js --cwd <tmp>/workspace codex -s named prompt ping
exit_code: 1
stdout: [error] RUNTIME: Failed to spawn agent command: acpx-proof-missing-agent
stderr: [acpx] session named (named-prompt-session) · <tmp>/workspace · agent needs reconnect
```
target.author: turbotheturtle
